ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Mira' — canonical. Blue-skinned humanoid with white spiky hair, red eyes, and white/gold chest-plated outfit is Mira's definitive published design. Dragon Ball Wiki confirms Mira is an artificial being from the Demon Realm with blue skin and white hair, confirmed Toriyama-designed. Card name plus visual match confirms identity.

trivia_bullets.text: Mira is an artificial being created by Towa from the Demon Realm. He is a composite of multiple warrior DNA strands — Earthling, Demon Realm race, Android components, and confirmed Saiyan DNA — built with the explicit goal of becoming the strongest being in the universe. His goal of collecting Goku's DNA drives the main plot of the Xenoverse games. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Mira, via WebSearch]
sources: Dragon Ball Wiki: Mira, via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Mira was confirmed as a Toriyama-designed character by Dragon Ball Xenoverse producer Masayuki Hirano. He originated in Dragon Ball Online (2010) and was the final boss of both Xenoverse 1 and Xenoverse 2's main stories. His chest emblem (teal, visible on the card) is consistent across all canonical Mira appearances. [CBR: 10 Things About Mira, via WebSearch]
sources: CBR: 10 Things About Mira, via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: As SD3-05, Mira is the fifth card in the Dark Invasion starter deck — positioned as the deck's power-declaration finisher after Trunks (SD3-03) and Masked Saiyan (SD3-04) establish the board. The starter deck's design mirrors the Xenoverse narrative: Masked Saiyan as the threat that draws out the heroes, Mira as the final boss lurking behind it. [WebSearch: DBS SD3 Dark Invasion product listing; WebSearch: Series 3 roundup strategy]
sources: WebSearch: DBS SD3 Dark Invasion product listing
